headerDisplay: Exposes contact sensors that close when the sun's azimuth and altitude fall within configured ranges and (optionally) cloud cover is below a threshold.
footerDisplay: Azimuth is measured in degrees from north (0° = north, 90° = east, 180° = south, 270° = west). Altitude is degrees above the horizon (0° = horizon, 90° = zenith). Negative altitudes mean the sun is below the horizon. Get a free OpenWeatherMap API key at https://openweathermap.org/appid.

openWeatherMapApiKey.title: OpenWeatherMap API Key
openWeatherMapApiKey.description: Optional. When provided, cloud cover is fetched every 10 minutes and used to gate the contact sensor. Get a free key at openweathermap.org.

pollInterval.title: Solar Sensor Poll Interval (seconds)
pollInterval.description: How often to recalculate the sun position (in seconds). Weather is always fetched every 10 minutes independently.

verboseLog.title: Verbose Logging
verboseLog.description: Log sun position, cloud cover, and sensor state at info level every poll interval.

name.title: Sensor Name
name.description: The name shown in HomeKit. Must be unique.

azimuthMin.title: Azimuth Min (°)
azimuthMin.description: Start of the azimuth window (degrees from north, clockwise).

azimuthMax.title: Azimuth Max (°)
azimuthMax.description: End of the azimuth window. If min > max the range wraps through north (0°).

cloudCoverMax.title: Max Cloud Cover (%)
cloudCoverMax.description: 0–100. Contact closes only when cloud cover is at or below this percentage. Requires an OpenWeatherMap API key. Ignored if no key is set.
